HomePage.title: Welcome to Valora
HomePage.description: This page gives you a simple overview of what the system already does and how it supports your daily work.

status.title: System ready to use
status.description: You can already navigate the workspace, organize your structure, and evolve your records safely.

workspace.title: Current workspace
workspace.description: You are in the protected area with an active session and everything prepared for a smooth workflow.

locale.title: Language and region
locale.description: The platform is already prepared to support multiple languages without losing navigation consistency.

entryFlow.title: Current flow
entryFlow.description: At sign-in, the system already identifies your context so you can continue from the right place without manual setup.

tenant.title: Organization and team
tenant.description: Define who is part of the operation and which role each person has in daily work.

location.title: Location structure
location.description: Organize your operation by units and hierarchy levels to keep a clear view of the whole.

ConfigurationPage.title: Organization, who can act, and which context
ConfigurationPage.description: Before day-to-day operations, Valora needs three things spelled out: which contracting organization this is (the tenant), who may act inside it (members and roles), and which slice of context data lives in, scopes: branches, projects, sites, whatever matches your operation. This screen is where you maintain that backbone; without it, records and permissions lack a clear frame of reference. The locations tree lives under its own sidebar entry and always follows the active scope you pick in the header.

organization.title: Tenant
organization.description: The contracting organization for this workspace: the name shown in the top bar, tenant switcher, and other surfaces. Here you align display name (what people see every day) and full legal or formal name when you need an official reference. Changes apply to the tenant active in your session; if you manage more than one, switch from the menu before editing.

member.title: Members
member.description: A member is a person tied to this tenant in the product, not just the sign-in account, but the link with role (master, administrator, member) and status. Everyone listed here can operate in this organization; roles and status control what they can change, including sensitive configuration.

scope.title: Scope
scope.description: A scope is a context unit inside the tenant: where records, and later the locations hierarchy, stay separated instead of mixed together. Think branch, job site, venue, or project,  the label is yours, but the job is always to isolate data that should not appear in one pile. The scope selected in the header is what applies when you work on locations and anything that depends on it.

field.title: Fields
field.description: Fields define value types used by rules in the active scope (for example integers or decimals). The list and editor follow the scope you pick in the header, the same pattern as locations.

action.title: Actions
action.description: Actions are business event kinds in the active scope (for example housing or mortality). Each has a friendly name per language and may have formulas attached. The list follows the header scope, the same pattern as fields.

MemberConfigurationPage.title: Members
MemberConfigurationPage.description: Edit the records of the members linked to the active tenant. Name, role, and status stay centralized in this directory.

list.empty: No members were found for the active tenant.

history.title: Change history
history.description: Review recent changes to member records: who changed what, when, and a clear summary of each update.

profile.displayNameHint: Used across the tenant's internal surfaces.
profile.nameHint: Use the most complete name for administrative reference.

invite.title: Invite
invite.description: New records start as a pending invite until the person accepts with the account for the email you enter.
invite.createHint: Save to register the invite. The person will see this tenant after signing in with a Google account that uses the same email.
invite.emailFieldHint: Enter the email that will receive the invite. First sign-in uses a Google account with that same address.

idle.title: Information
idle.description: How to use this panel: pick a member in the list on the left to edit their record, or use New to register an invite when you have permission.
idle.selectHint: Click a member in the list or use the New button to register an email invite.
idle.emptyHint: There are no members in this tenant and your current permission does not allow registering invites.

access.title: Access
access.description: Role and status control the member's reach inside this tenant.
access.memberEmailHint: Invite and first-login address. You can change it anytime; it does not have to match the Google account email after the link is established.

MemberConfigurationPage.readOnlyNotice: You can review the member directory, but only master or admin members can edit records.
MemberConfigurationPage.protectedRecordNotice: This record is protected by the current permissions. Select another member to edit.

ScopeConfigurationPage.title: Scopes
ScopeConfigurationPage.description: Edit the minimum structural foundation of the active tenant. Each scope defines an initial slice that will support later records and modules.

FieldConfigurationPage.title: Fields
FieldConfigurationPage.description: Adjust here the names of the information you measure or record in events. For every name (such as quantity or amount), you tell the system how to read the content: whole numbers, values with cents, yes or no, and similar choices. That applies when you build calculation rules and when you record events, so values stay consistent and are not misread.
FieldConfigurationPage.empty: No fields were found for the active scope and your current permission does not allow creating new ones.

list.emptyScope: Create a scope before editing fields.
list.missingCurrentScope: Set an active scope from the header selector before editing fields.
list.loadError: Could not load fields for this scope. Try again.

history.title: Change history
history.description: Review recent changes to field definitions for the active scope: who changed what, when, and a summary of each update.

fieldName.label: Field name
fieldName.hint: Field name. Enter a short, easy-to-understand name, for example: quantity, value, growth percentage. This name is used in formulas, charts, and reports.

info.title: Information
info.description: Summary of the field in focus: stable identifier and type saved on the server.
info.createHint: You are defining a new field for the active scope. Save to register it in the directory.

ActionConfigurationPage.title: Actions
ActionConfigurationPage.description: Define here the kinds of operations or measurements that become events in the active scope (for example housing or mortality). For each action you provide a clear name; that name appears when you build rules and record events.

history.title: Change history
history.description: Review recent changes to action definitions for the active scope: who changed what, when, and a summary of each update.

actionName.label: Action name
actionName.hint: Short, easy-to-understand name used in formulas, screens, and reports.

info.title: Information
info.description: Summary of the action in focus on the server.
info.createHint: You are defining a new action for the active scope. Save to register it in the directory.

ActionConfigurationPage.readOnlyNotice: You can review actions for the active scope, but only master or admin members can create or edit rule definitions.

formulas.title: Formulas
formulas.description:
Formulas are calculation steps applied when events for this action are processed. Each step is text where you can write numbers, operations (such as + and *), and references to information in the scope.

Type @ to open a list and insert a field:n reference. Type # to open the same list and insert an input:n reference. The selected name appears underlined in the text and you can keep typing as usual.

The list order is execution order, top to bottom. Drag the handle beside a formula to reorder before saving.
formulas.exampleList:
Math examples:
Addition: x + y
Subtraction: x - y
Multiplication: x * y
Division: x / y
Integer division: x // y
Modulo: x % y
Power: x ** 2
Parentheses: (x + y) * z
Rounding: round((x * y) / z, 2)
Minimum: min(x, y, z)
Maximum: max(x, y, z)
Absolute value: abs(x - y)
Decimal: Decimal("0.2") * x

Conditional examples:
Simple condition: x if y > 0 else 0
Equality condition: x + 10 if y == 1 else x - 10
Max/min condition: max(x, y) if z > 100 else min(x, y)
Safe division condition: round(x / y, 2) if y != 0 else 0
And condition: x if (y > 0 and z > 0) else 0

Other supported features:
Comparisons: x == y, x != y, x > y, x >= y, x < y, x <= y
Logical: x > 0 and y > 0, x > 0 or y > 0, not (x > y)
Date: x if data_ref > date(2026, 1, 1) else y
Functions: abs, min, max, round, Decimal, and date
formulas.removeAriaLabel: Mark formula for removal on save
formulas.empty: No formulas yet. Use new to add one.
formulas.loadError: Could not load formulas. Try again.
formulas.statementRequired: Each active formula needs a non-empty statement.

validationError.formula_invalid_assignment: Each step must have exactly one assignment: what receives the value, an equals sign, then the calculation. Check that there is only one assignment equals sign and that neither side is empty.
validationError.formula_invalid_target: Only the field that should receive the result may appear to the left of the equals sign (a field:n reference), nothing else.
validationError.formula_unknown_field_id: The formula references information that does not exist in this scope. Adjust the chosen items or the text.
validationError.formula_expression_invalid: The calculation to the right of the equals sign could not be validated. Check parentheses, operators, and allowed functions (such as abs, min, max, round, Decimal, and date).

LocationConfigurationPage.title: Locations
LocationConfigurationPage.description: In this panel you see your location tree. The hierarchy appears on the left; beside it is the form to edit the name and other information about the location, plus the history panel. Select a location in the tree to edit it, or click the New button to create a new subdivision at that location.

list.empty: No locations were found for the selected scope.
list.emptyScope: Create a scope first before editing the location hierarchy.
list.missingCurrentScope: Set an active scope from the header selector before editing the location hierarchy.

history.description: Review recent changes to your location hierarchy: who changed what, when, and a summary of each update.

identity.nameHint: Use a short name to identify this location. For example: Willow instead of Willow Farm, Brookside instead of Brookside Poultry, or East instead of East Field.
identity.displayNameHint: Enter the full name of the location. For example: Willow Farm instead of Willow, Brookside Poultry instead of Brookside, or East Field instead of just East.

structure.title: Information
structure.description: Summary of the location in focus, with the level in the hierarchy and the order among siblings of the same location.
structure.levelHint: Counted from the scope root (1 = root).
structure.orderHintEdit: Used when listing locations at the same level.
structure.orderHintCreate: Assigned when you save the new location.

LocationConfigurationPage.dragDropHint: Drag a handle to reorder before or after a sibling, or drop on a row to move under that location.

UnityConfigurationPage.title: Productive units
UnityConfigurationPage.description: This panel shows the productive-unit tree for the scope. The hierarchy is on the left; beside it is the form to edit the name and description, plus the history panel. Select an item in the tree to edit it, or use the New button to add a child under the chosen item.

TenantConfigurationPage.description: These fields identify the contracting organization in the workspace, top bar, and lists. Changes apply to the active tenant of your session.

LoginPage.title: Your business presented in numbers you stand behind with purpose and provenance.
LoginPage.description: One source of truth that grows with the company, with aligned versions, improving results and lowering risk. Sign in with Google to open the right organizational context for this session.

notice.authRequired: Sign in to access this area.

workspace.title: See the whole, drill down when it matters
workspace.description: Room for the team to execute, and visibility for you to steer the next step.

traceability.title: Stand behind the numbers
traceability.description: Changes stay on record. What you show partners or auditors rests on history and context.

nextStep.title: After you sign in
nextStep.description: Quick entry to your environment. With several tenants or invites, you pick where to work this session.

form.title: Access Valora
form.accessPolicy: First visit: we help you set tenant, team, and scope so the company can get started.
form.googleUnavailable: Google sign-in is unavailable: the public Google client identifier has not been configured in this environment yet.
